title: Node.js Developer
description: Step by step guide to becoming a Node.js developer in 2025

topics.name: / Watch
topics.description: The `--watch` flag in Node.js is a powerful feature introduced in Node.js version 19 that enables automatic reloading of your Node.js application whenever changes are detected in the specified files.

topics.name: __dirname
topics.description: The `__dirname` in a node script returns the path of the folder where the current JavaScript file resides. `__filename` and `__dirname` are used to get the filename and directory name of the currently executing file.

topics.name: __filename
topics.description: The `__filename` in Node.js returns the filename of the executed code. It gives the absolute path of the code file. The following approach covers implementing `__filename` in the Node.js project.

topics.name: Axios
topics.description: Axios is a promise-based HTTP Client for node.js and the browser. Used for making requests to web servers. On the server-side it uses the native node.js http module, while on the client (browser) it uses XMLHttpRequests.

topics.name: Building / Consuming Apis
topics.description: API is the acronym for Application Programming Interface, which is a software intermediary that allows two applications to talk to each other.

topics.name: Callbacks

topics.name: Chalk Package

topics.description: The child\_process module gives the node the ability to run the child process, established through IPC (inter-process communication) by accessing operating system commands.

topics.name: Chokidar
topics.description: Chokidar is a fast open-source file watcher for node. js. You give it a bunch of files, it watches them for changes and notifies you every time an old file is edited; or a new file is created.

topics.name: Cli Progress
topics.description: CLI-Progress is a package that provides a custom progress bar for CLI applications.

topics.description: The Cluster module allows you to easily create child processes that each runs simultaneously on their own single thread, to handle workloads among their application threads.

topics.description: Commander is a light-weight, expressive, and powerful command-line framework for node.js. with Commander.js you can create your own command-line interface (CLI).
